what_it_is: TRIZ contradiction matrix used for General engineering, AI-enhanced innovation tools, Product design and development problem framing.

when_to_use.ideal_user_profile: Engineering teams using AI-assisted innovation tools, Chinese-speaking TRIZ practitioners, product designers seeking a simplified TRIZ entry point, software developers building TRIZ-based ideation platforms, and educators teaching introductory TRIZ courses.
best_for: Quick AI-assisted contradiction identification from natural language problem descriptions
Weight-vs-Strength trade-offs in mechanical and structural design
Speed-vs-Precision contradictions in manufacturing and automation
Energy consumption-vs-Efficiency trade-offs in sustainable design
Building or extending AI-powered TRIZ tools with NLP parameter detection
not_suitable_for: Comprehensive contradiction matrix lookups requiring high coverage
Pharmaceutical governance or healthcare service quality problems
Academic research requiring empirically validated matrices
Problems involving domain-specific parameters not in the 18-parameter set
Situations requiring provenance and traceability to patent databases
when_to_use.prefer_over_alternatives_when: Use this matrix when building or using AI-powered TRIZ tools that need NLP-friendly parameter definitions with keyword arrays, when working in bilingual Chinese/English contexts, or when the classical 39x39 matrix feels overwhelming and a modern, simplified parameter set is desired. It is the only matrix in the collection with implementation-ready keyword arrays and principle-level example/application documentation.

strengths.area: Simplified, modern, accessible entry point to TRIZ
strengths.why: With 18 clearly named parameters (Weight, Strength, Speed, Precision, Cost, Quality, Complexity, Volume, Safety, Efficiency, Reliability, Maintenance, Automation, Functionality, Energy consumption, Convenience, Intelligence, Performance) and bilingual Chinese/English support, this matrix is far more approachable than the classical 39x39 for newcomers and AI-assisted tools.

Each parameter includes Chinese keyword arrays for NLP matching, and the system is designed to work with AI keyword-based parameter detection as a fallback when no matrix entry exists. This makes it uniquely suited for integration into LLM-powered or chatbot-driven innovation tools.

strengths.area: Modern engineering vocabulary including automation and intelligence
strengths.why: Parameters like Automation, Intelligence, Convenience, and Energy consumption reflect contemporary engineering concerns absent from the 1960s-era classical matrix, making the contradiction framing more natural for modern product development.

strengths.area: Rich principle documentation with practical examples
strengths.why: All 40 principles include detailed descriptions, real-world examples, implementation steps, benefits, and category labels in both Chinese and English -- far richer than any other matrix in the collection.

weaknesses.why: Only 16 entries populate a potential 18x18 space (306 possible combinations). The vast majority of parameter pairs have no matrix entry, forcing reliance on AI fallback rather than systematic contradiction resolution.

Unlike the peer-reviewed drug_safety and healthcare_servqual matrices, this is a community/open-source project without published validation studies. The 16 entries may reflect author intuition rather than systematic patent or case analysis.

Parameters 15-18 (Energy consumption, Convenience, Intelligence, Performance) appear in the matrix but have no keyword arrays, suggesting they were added to fill gaps rather than being part of the original systematic design.
